title: Diagnosis and initial assessment
html:
• Measure BP accurately and confirm hypertension with ABPM/HBPM where appropriate.
  • Classify stage and check for urgent red flags.
  • Perform baseline tests: urine ACR/haematuria, HbA1c, U&E, creatinine/eGFR, lipids, ECG, fundoscopy.
  • Assess formal CVD risk and possible secondary causes.

title: Primary care management (detailed)
html:
• Offer repeated lifestyle intervention: weight, diet, sodium reduction, exercise, alcohol reduction, smoking cessation.
  • Step 1: ACEi/ARB or CCB according to age/diabetes/family origin; thiazide-like diuretic if CCB not tolerated.
  • Step 2: combine 2 classes after adherence and dose review.
  • Step 3: ACEi/ARB + CCB + thiazide-like diuretic.
  • Step 4 resistant HTN: confirm with ABPM/HBPM, reassess adherence/postural drop/secondary causes; consider spironolactone if suitable.
  • Monitor renal function/electrolytes and adverse effects after initiation and dose changes.

title: Monitoring, targets and referral
html:
• Clinic targets: <140/90 if under 80; <150/90 if 80 or over (individualise for frailty/postural hypotension).
  • Use home BP monitoring to support control and adherence.
  • Review every 4–6 weeks during titration, then at least annually once stable.
  • Refer non-urgently for suspected secondary causes, resistant hypertension, or complex management uncertainty.
